comments.title: Advice for social relations in decline
comments.content:
I'm facing a challenging set of social relationships. Basically, I've spent the last decade or more preparing for decline, mentally and financially. It wasn't and isn't easy, but for some reason I'm ok mental and physical shape, and so are my family.

However, most of my family and friends are not, and jealousy and occasional nastiestnsss are their response. It's tough because I care about people and don't want the life of loneliness for my family, but nor is being dragged down by emotionally drowning people a good idea.

Times are tough, advice gratefully appreciated.

replies.title: Re: Advice for social relations in decline
replies.content: I don't recommend asking someone with Aspergers syndrome, like me, for advice in social relationships! For that reason I'm going to hand this over to the commentariat for their advice.

replies.title: Re: Advice for social relations in decline
replies.content: Sometimes a bit of distance does everyone good imo, and sometimes after a bit of distance you can reconnect with greater levels of courtesy and reinstate social visits that bring the benefits with less emotional burden if both parties are open to it. In my experience if you feel the toxicity is building it's better to pull back or it can get to a point that the relationship breaks even if you were dedicated to keeping things going, but that's something only you can gauge.

replies.title: Re: Advice for social relations in decline
replies.content:
There's a classic on improving relations called How to Win Friends and Influence People. Things like knowing child names, birthdays, and following up on favorite topics. Overall good advice!

Even when people see this goes against my nature, they recognize and appreciate the effort.

replies.title: Re: Advice for social relations in decline
replies.content: Honestly, you can't really do much about the jealousy and nastiness of others.  You might very well have to cut those people out of your life, or severely limit interactions with them.  A small but supportive social network is better than a large social network that mistreats you for being wise enough to prepare for decline.  If that isn't tenable for you, for whatever reason, I just recommend trying to be as Stoic as possible about it.  In fact, just being Stoic in general might very well be a good idea.  Decline is accelerating, and a lot of nastiness is about to arrive.

replies.title: Re: Advice for social relations in decline
replies.content:
One just has to face up to basic unregenerate human nature, which - mostly - isn't very pretty, especially in a corrupt and decadent society like ours.

If you happen to make better decisions and prosper, you will generally only invite spite, envy and malice, because your very existence will be a living reproach to those who are doing badly.

Far from being seen as a potential role model, you will quite likely be hated; in really dire times you may well be robbed, ruined or killed - and by those closest to you in blood.

The best people (so few!) will reflect, admire and emulate; the mediocre and the worst will see your prudence and foresight as offensive.

Particularly dismaying if it is family: but the Great Descent is going to be a dangerous and difficult challenge!

How to cope? Entertain only the most limited expectations and hopes for others; be grimly realistic and be on you guard; above all reveal as little of your thoughts and actions as possible, and find some way to insulate yourself mentally and spiritually against the negativity which will be directed at you.

The others will most probably not see the light or reform themselves, so don't waste energy hoping for it.

I find ancient texts like the Havamal quite useful. Much good advice for dangerous times - and it IS going to be very perilous.

Get used to the idea that you may also have to kill to survive, and practice for it.

Being as fit and strong as possible will pay great dividends in terms of feeling good, and facing the reality of inevitable Evil.

Good luck!

replies.title: Re: Advice for social relations in decline
replies.content: Set up internal boundaries and a focus on the here and now. Other people will be negative at times - but doing an affirmation practice to develop the internal strength to focus on the here and now and let others petty emotions slide is a good start.

replies.title: Re: Advice for social relations in decline
replies.content:
People who predict bad things are often, later, accused of causing them when they appear. be aware of that!

The Grey Badger

replies.title: Re: Advice for social relations in decline
replies.content:
Hear, hear!

I'm lucky in that my family is 100% on board with the no-V, batten down the hatches for hard times program.

Were I to find myself in the OP's situation, there are a couple of steps I'd take:

1) stop talking about anything like that in front of them.
2) Pray for them: each, individually, every day, that they be blessed, protected from harm, and their needs be met. I don't know whether this does anything for them, but in the past I have found it extremely useful for easing contentious relationships. Possibly because it modifies my perspective on it.

comments.title: A magical-medical question
comments.content:
Hello JMG, thanks for another Magic Monday.

I currently suffer from quite the silly ailment -- at 43 years old I have a recurring case of hydrocele, a baby's disease, where liquid accumulates at one of the inner layers of the scrotum, making it grow like a balloon. I have, of course, treated this through the use of traditional medicine -- went to the doctor, underwent surgery. The thing came back. I'm all set for another surgery. However I wonder if there's something else behind this: I'm not in peace with my sexuality and love life, and an embiggened scrotum filled with "unspent energy" is an image I could use to describe it if I decided to be harsh and not very poetic. Could an energetic or subtle cause be behind this? It should NOT have reocurred after surgery, it was a corrective procedure.

Should I try an alchemist? Herbologist? I just think about sex/fire being flooded with sadness/water and end up imagining with spagyrics or even some sort of tea could be of help.

Thanks! :) And I say it again: I am asking about this but going through secular medical treatment normally.

replies.title: Re: A magical-medical question
replies.content:
In the United States it is illegal for someone other than a licensed health care practitioner to offer advice on any health condition. It's called practicing medicine without a license, and people do hard time if they get caught. No, you don't have to charge money to be accused of it -- there are people who have been prosecuted for practicing medicine without a license because a friend asked them what to do about a cold and they suggested herbs.

That law is also tolerably often used, btw, as a basis for entrapment, especially as a means to target political undesirables. Thus I tend to be very careful about such things -- and for that reason I am not in a position to help you. Sorry.

replies.title: Re: A magical-medical question
replies.content:
Energetic and subtle causes coexist with everything in physical manifestation. It's not so much that they're the causal root "behind" what happens physically as it is that energies always tend to manifest together and express themselves on all the planes, from the densest to the subtlest. Are there subtle energies intermixed in your condition... as well as every other situation in your life? Well, yes, of course there are. Would working and playing with those subtle energies be able to cause changes in all the planes, right down to the densest? Again, yes. Will you personally be able to impact what you're experiencing right now by working and playing with those subtle energies? That remains unknown. If you want to know, you will have to give it a try to see how it manifests for you.

Since human scrotums hang down just under our root chakras, the root from which our coiled kundalini rises, either upwards or outwards depending on how sexually roused we are at the time, that chakra might be a good place to begin looking for energetic blockages in your body. Whether those blockages would end up feeling more like an over-coiled spring, a stick up your butt, bottled up juices, impeded energies that won’t flow, or something completely unpredictable is a discovery you get to make for yourself. All of us end up walking around with an enormous number of energetic parasites swirling around our genitals — that’s just part of being an adult human. Learning to get over our understandable squeamishness so that we can explore those energy drains and begin clearing them out of our ecosystems is a challenging, but highly therapeutic choice. And, yes, some of those parasites are horrifically revolting and exceedingly uncomfortable to fully dispel; however, it is always very much worth the effort.

At the risk of sounding like John Michael, have you tried journaling about your condition, writing out everything that you can possibly imagine might be contributing to it in any way whatsoever? That is to say, without editing any of the thoughts that may pop into your head, no matter how crazy or unrelated your conscious mind may want to judge them. Actually, the more resistant or doubtful or censoring you feel about writing something down, the more likely that those are the very thoughts underneath which the greatest treasures can be found buried.

There are lots of different techniques that can help get the subconscious juices flowing, including dialoguing with the infirmity, asking it why it picked you, making a list of your fears or grievances or repressed fantasies, brainstorming whom in your life your condition may be harming or benefiting, reviewing the concerns and choices that were stymieing you right before the condition first manifested or re-manifested, detailing every nuance of every emotion you're feeling about your condition or about sex or potency or intimacy, personifying your genitalia and letting them tell their own story, etc. A complete list of potential topics for exploration and journaling would be endless, so just pick some angle you find promising and dive in.

Your sex/fire getting flooded with sadness/water is a great image to work with. Why is your flame getting doused? Where is all the water coming from? What were you doing to keep your fire safely tended and contained before this condition had to take over that role? How were you managing to drain away any excessive fluidity before this condition arrived, revealing just how important that self-regulation had actually been? What parts of your larger self have become too enflamed, dried out, or brittle, and could benefit from unblocking those entrapped fluids? What parts of your larger self need to be roused and lit up to burn off some pressurized steam. After all, what is pressurized steam but water confined and trapped in heat? And I promise you, being trapped in heat really sucks balls! Just ask any yowling indoor cat.

— Christophe

replies.title: Re: A magical-medical question
replies.content:
I have done a quick search in the Materia Medica,- just through the remedies that start with the letter A,- and found 9 which list hydrocele as a condition for which they are useful. Just 4 of them further specified "in children", which makes me think that the other 5 are prescribed usually but not exclusively to babies.

I will not mention any of those here for the reasons stated by JMG (just in case one day I have to travel to the US). So my advice is for you to find a licensed practitioner of Homeopathy in your place of residence (or somewhere nearby you can travel easily) and have them make a prescription that best fits your full set of symptoms.

replies.content:
Just want to pop in and offer some advice on d):

I wouldn't say I've 'beaten' porn addiction - it's an insidious ailment that hooks into something very beautiful and natural (loving physical union between two people that grows, whether a new life or love and connection) and makes it very ugly and unnatural (a commodified spectacle that consumes time, energy and focus). As many men born after 1985, I was exposed early and frequently and it's going to be a struggle for a time.

However, I have made a lot of progress, and I want to help where I can.

Magical literacy has helped a lot; the will training and concentration afforded by meditation sort of 'snapped' me out of the hypnosis that porn puts upon you. You also begin to see and feel the malicious energy coursing through that industry, which made it unbearable for me to utilize. I don't know if that will work for you, so I will share some insights and tips.

I don't know your situation, but the first thing I had to do was unwind the layers of intensity. I don't want to disrespect JMG's space, so I won't go into specifics, but suffice it to say that I had to progressively watch less degenerate and less intense performances. If it didn't 'satisfy,' that's fine. Let it go for the day and try again next time the urge grabs you, which leads to my next point.

Masturbation cessation never worked for me. The whole 'nofap' thing gives off culty, peer-pressure vibes, and while I'm sure the structure works for some, it did not work for me. Instead, holding off until I absolutely had to do it or I could not continue functioning normally, and then using pornography that is lesser in intensity than last time. Essentially, get hungry enough that something a bit softer/more natural satisfies.

A big aspect of porn addiction is using it to fill time or get the dopamine hit, rather than satisfy a biological need. On this point, my main goals were:

1. Less variety. I would find one actress, preferably one video, for the entire session. The amount of porn available is insane; you or I or anyone can see in a single night more beautiful young people naked than the most powerful man in the history of the world, pre-internet, saw in his entire life.
2. Try to find videos that simulate natural intercourse. PoV.
3. Your aim is to get to a place where watching someone else around your age masturbating alone is completely satisfying and then perhaps try masturbating without porn at all.

Eventually, you want to find a way to channel the energy you're burning off with masturbation into something else; whether a partner that you build a true and loving connection with, or a creative endeavor perhaps.

This does not address the very real spiritual/etheric risks involved in porn consumption (and masturbation in general). JMG has an article on the main blog about it, I highly recommend you read it. Helped me significantly.

It is important as well to remember that there is no free lunch. My personal belief is that free porn takes more than just the ad revenue. It might take it from the poor, suffering people appearing in the videos or it might take it from you.

I wish you well, my friend.

comments.title: Dion (Violet Firth) Fortune's Inner Contacts
comments.content:
Greetings JMK!,

I've recently given some thought to the question of the Inner Plane
Contacts that Dion Fortune claimed were giving some impetus to her
society/order prior to and after WW1. Perhaps this was prompted by
the relatively recent passing of Gareth Knight (Basil Wilby), who did
a great deal of work putting her story together for the present, and
future generations?

That said, one of these contacts was ostensibly a young(ish) British
soldier who claimed to have given his life during WW1, at the Battle
of the Somme, I believe. The details of his Life and Death that he
supplied, and were subsequently made available by Dion Fortune turned
out to be more or less unverifiable if I recall correctly?

Finally, here's the question I want to get your thought/s on:
In your opinion were these Inner Plane contacts that were purportedly
working on behalf of her order totally Subjective to Herself alone, or
Objective in the sense that other members were also able to communicate with them?

Thanks,
JDB

replies.title: Re: Dion (Violet Firth) Fortune's Inner Contacts
replies.content:
Other members of the FIL communicated with Fortune's contacts on a regular basis. That's tolerably well documented in books on Fortune and her school. You might be interested to know that there's a doctoral dissertation on the subject of Fortune's contacts:

https://ore.exeter.ac.uk/repository/handle/10036/41936

replies.title: Re: Dion (Violet Firth) Fortune's Inner Contacts
replies.content: Fascinating. Do you happen to know of any other instances of the official academy taking the ideas of occultism seriously?

replies.title: Re: Dion (Violet Firth) Fortune's Inner Contacts
replies.content: Theer are several such places in the academic world.  In the UK, the University of Exeter has a Centre for Magic and Esotericism which offers masters and doctoral degrees.  There are similar programs at the University of Amsterdam and at the École pratique des hautes études in Paris.  In the United States, Rice University (Houston, TX) offers a PhD program in "Mysticism, Gnosticism & Esotericism" through its Religious Studies department.  I know faculty from Amsterdam (Wouter J. Hanegraaff) and from Rice University (Claire Fanger), and think very highly of their scholarship and of them as people.

replies.title: Re: Dion (Violet Firth) Fortune's Inner Contacts
replies.content:
Parapsychology was most academically credible, even if still fringe, between maybe 1935-1985. But then parapsychology isn't entirely in the same paradigm as occultism. Compared to occultists, the go-to explanatory metaphors that parapsychologists tend to look to are more physical-machine- and legal-system-flavored and less symbolism- and narrative-flavored.

There was an earlier period where some academics were studying the manifestations associated with Spiritualism, but I don't know much about it.

replies.title: Re: Dion (Violet Firth) Fortune's Inner Contacts
replies.content:
What Robert said. ;-)

More generally, the academic study of occultism has been a rising trend since the 1960s, when Frances Yates and a handful of others forced it into the academic limelight. One of the really bright spots in current occultism is that there are so many good translations and editions of old magical texts, and so many worthwhile scholarly studies of magic -- both of which were rarer than shoggoth's teeth not that long ago.

comments.title: Mental Plane and Egregores
comments.content:
a) After reading your post on the polarities of subtle bodies, I’ve been pondering over the mental plane/sheath and how it interacts with inspiration. You mentioned how male artists (with female sheaths) often need to receive inspiration from female companionship. However, I’ve known artists who seemed to receive inspiration from immaterial beings. This wouldn’t be strange, except the “beings” in question are often fictional characters from (popular) media/stories they’ve consumed. Their “muse” would therefore be an egregore (I believe that’s the correct term?) that seems to be fertilizing their mental sheath, which implies said egregore possesses a male sheath.
An immaterial being having subtle bodies seems straightforward. What’s confusing, in the case of fictional characters, is that the “being” was human-made. This raises multiple questions: is the assumption that egregores possess their own sheaths correct? If so, what determines the egregore’s mental sheath? Does it mimic the polarity of the original creator/author? If not, could an author create their own “muse” by forming an egregore with an opposing polarity?

To clarify, I’m talking specifically about inspiration rather than imagination (on the astral), although, I suppose creating an egregore this way would involve the astral?

b) Any advice for someone whose polarities are unbalanced? I am male, but have (from what I understand) a female etheric/astral/mental body.

replies.title: Re: Mental Plane and Egregores
replies.content:
1) Egregors don't have mental sheaths because they aren't persons, but they can certainly exist and function on the mental plane, so long as they have some connection to the realm of meaning and value. Most good works of art and literature achieve this as a matter of course; it's the connection to the mental plane, in fact, that distinguishes real art, literature, etc. from schlock. Any egregore with a mental plane aspect, if it's charged by the emotions and thoughts of fans, can function as a source of inspiration.

2) That's rare enough that I haven't encountered any discussion of it.

Also, have you (or others) written about how the mental plane distinguishes real art from everything else? The concept of “real art” seems topical, considering the AI boom (though I’m sure you’re tired of discussing the subject…)

b) Unfortunate. Does that apply to all lopsided combinations?

replies.title: Re: Mental Plane and Egregores

I'm physically male/etherically female and I have just figured out that I most likely have a female astral body (I assume I'm mentally female as well). I won't presume to offer advice just yet, since I haven't been able to use this information to create a life that makes sense for me as yet.

Always on the outer, drifting to the edge and quitting in dissatisfaction with jobs within a couple of years, never feeling like I was part of anything. Sliding through seven years of tertiary study without forming a network.

I thought I was autistic (definitely have ADHD) because of the apparent social ineptness and have lived in extreme cognitive dissonance for years. Being very empathetic, I actually perceive a lot of what is wordlessly going on with people, so it was a recipe for mental illness to imagine that there was all this information being shared that I wasn't perceiving.

Having this explanation has liberated me from a LOT. It explains so much. There's nothing wrong with me, there are simply no social niches that fit me and relatively few people that I would be able to properly connect with. I can vibe with women. There's almost always sexual interest in it from me but I don't think I excite them in the right way. I've been surprised more than once when sustained eye contact and lively conversation did not equate to any interest on their end. Harrumph.

My 15 year relationship with my ex-wife ended a couple of years ago for reasons that can be traced to this. I just don't have the wiring to instinctively play the roles that people with male bodies are expected to play these days, although as JMG posted, that's a shaleshow anyway.

Good luck figuring out what works for you.

replies.title: On the rosary
replies.content:
#4: TL;DR It is probably Ok, but if you are interested to work with the Rosary it is possible to do the full set of days/Planets.

Please let me explain, there are 15 Mysteries[1] that you should meditate on while you pray the Rosary; those are divided in 3 groups of five: Joyful, Sorrowful and Glorious. The Joyful are prayed on Mondays and Thursdays, the Sorrowful on Tuesdays and Fridays, the Glorious on Wednesdays and Saturdays. Let's leave aside Sundays for now.

According to Agostino Taumaturgo[2] the alignment of the Mysteries follow an astrologic worldview. It is obvious why you want to meditate on the Joyful Mysteries on Jupiter's day; I am not sure about Mondays because I cannot place "Luna" (definitively not Hera, and Diane's energy seems off); and from a Christian point of view[3] I'd say we are talking of the masculine principle of The Holy Providence (a very jovial aspect shared by all the Persons of the Divine Trinity) and the feminine principle of the Theotokos.

On the Sorrowful Mysteries, the planets are Mars and Venus, and in this polarity we find the sexual aspect more profoundly. Those are also the mysteries that deal with suffering and death, so they are the most human ones. I think that from the Christian point of view we are revering the original human couple, Adam and Eve, and in them seeing the reflection of the more earthly side of Jesus and his mother Mary, daughter of Joachim and Anne.

For the Glorious Mysteries, Taumaturgo points that they are to be prayed in the days or Mercury and Saturn. Here the duality is not along sexual lines, but along the age axis: Mercury being the bright youthful, range of possibilities, and Saturn playing the part of the ancient wise, fully aware of hard limits. As for Sundays, the author claims that you are to rotate through the whole range of Mysteries so that you do not get imbalanced by devoting one day to only one set of them (probably he meant 5 at a time, through 3 weeks; but I've heard of people who prayed the whole 15 Mysteries in one go, it would probably take a full hour and is better suited for the day of rest).

Here I deviate from his teaching because I always learned since childhood that Glorious Mysteries are of the Sunday too. It is as if we are invited to break the binary into a ternary, so the obvious Christian attribution would be the Most Holy Trinity. The Christ is the Solar logos, obviously, and the harsh but fair Old-Testament Yahweh, Lord of the Host, would be very well suited for Saturdays. That would live the Paraclitus, who touches directly the hearth of Men, as the Mercurial energy in the ternary.

[1] Those are the traditional 15 Mysteries; multiplied by 10 Hail-Marys give you 150, the same number as with the Psalms. This relationship have been disrupted by Pope Francis' new Luminous Mysteries, in which I am not particularly interested (though I do not claim they are evil or anything).

[2] The core of this elaboration comes from Taumaturgo's article as interpreted by me. However...

[3] ... the correspondences to Christian holy persons are my own. Those are the results from my attempt to square the practice learned in childhood, of praying Glorious Mysteries on Sundays.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
Keep in mind that the stonecutters were of three different degrees of initiation. The first one was an entered apprentice; his job was to focus on the stone as a stone, to learn that basic but absolutely necessary task thoroughly, so that he understood the nature of stone and thus what could and could not be done with it. The second was a fellow craft; he had finished the 'prentice work of learning the nature of stone, and so could get to work on refining each stone so it filled its proper place in the wall. The third was a master mason, and knew enough about building to understand the secret geometries that make a cathedral work.

The stuff that you think of as preliminaries, to be gotten out of the way, are the foundation for everything else. It's not a matter of clearing your plate, it's a matter of doing the most important work of all. I know it doesn't feel like that, especially because it's also very, very dull. I remember a book on Zen that influenced me a lot, many years ago, commenting that doing Zen meditation is like polishing a piece of jade that you can neither see nor feel. Only when it's perfectly smooth and the light glints off it can you begin to glimpse it!

comments.title: Catholic Vampires
comments.content:
Dear J.M. Greer,

When we look at the vampirization process, it seems to have begun with very good intentions - for a chief or shaman to continue dwelling in the land and doing things to benefit the tribe; this degenerated into people just being scared of losing their personalities in death. So it originated as something like a potent version of a Catholic saint - a dead person who will get blessings and benefits done for the tribe. In its degenerate form, it just created a bunch of parasites. So I was wondering, do we see a survival of the better version of vampirism in the Catholic saint process? Leaving aside the other categories of Catholic saints, who are actually gods, goddesses, Daimons, nature spirits, land beings, etc, mashed into one category. If the etheric body is well-preserved through spiritual practice (that's why we see Catholic saint bodies that don't decompose), and it's also fed through prayers and devotions, the saint (maybe we can call it a Catholic vampire) can continue functioning to benefit and bless people. Moreover, it seems these people are no longer attached to their personalities and passions, which makes them safe to work with.
The practical follow-up question to that would be, if one of their sanctified people dies in, say, North America, shouldn't we not embalm them? This would destroy their etheric shell.

Regards,

Josh Rout

replies.title: Re: Catholic Vampires
replies.content:
Good! Yes, but there's another factor: the saint isn't dependent on the preservation of his or her body. Plenty of powerful saints were burnt to ashes, or had their bodies chopped up in small pieces to be distributed as holy relics all over medieval Europe, or their body did the normal thing and moldered away to dust.

The point of the ancient tradition was to use the etheric shell only temporarily, until the soul can stabilize itself on higher planes. That's why bones were taken out of the long barrows back in megalithic times and used as sacred relics -- the guardian of the tribe didn't need them, though his or her influence radiated from them as the influence of a saint radiates from his relics.

With regard to embalming, the best thing to do is to wait at least three days before embalming or cremating the body. That allows the etheric body to separate completely from the material body. Once that's happened, the material body can be disposed of, or if it's the body of a saint, preserved as a source of relics.

comments.title: More on magic and spirituality
comments.content:
Following on my previous week's question on the difference between magic and spirituality (your answer was clarifying, thanks), magician Damien Echols once said in an interview (https://youtu.be/WQBDhSolHAM?t=26) that "the Bible is the greatest book of magic ever written, once you know how to read it". What do you make of that?

It's just that I've got this impression at times that some magicians see magic as this thing that is above spirituality, like spirituality is a subfield of magic. Certainly Aleister Crowley gives that impression in his novel Moonchild, with his magical theory that spirit is this one thing that is penetrating into the physical universe (and I suppose also with this whole thing of him making up a religion), and Éliphas Lévi also with his speculation that the religion of the future for the West is a fusion of Christianity with Buddhism.

You clearly don't see it that way, though I'm not sure why Dion Fortune's The Cosmic Doctrine is magic and not spirituality, since it does deal with states above the mental. Is it because of the qualification that it's all meant to train the mind, and that it's not at all an absolute truth?

replies.title: Re: More on magic and spirituality
replies.content:
1) It's a poor excuse for a holy book that can only be read in one way! Back in the Middle Ages it was standard teaching that the Bible should be read in four different ways -- the literal, as an account of events that happened; the moral, as a set of ethical instructions; the allegorical, as an account of theology in symbolic form; and the analogical, as a set of instructions for mystical practice. That leaves out the fifth, or magical sense, which is the one Damien was talking about. These senses, of course, align with the four elements:

Literal - Earth
Moral - Water
Allegorical - Air
Magical - Fire
Anagogical - Spirit

2) Yeah, I know, some occultists think that. I disagree. I see occultism as the middle ground between science and spirituality. As for the Cos. Doc., yes, exactly; it's mental training useful for occultists.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
1) If you're going to proceed with divination you will have to overcome the habit of superstition; it's one of the major barriers to any occult practice. My usual advice is to ask something like "What do I need to understand about the things I experience today?" I do that daily, keep track of the results, and review my readings the next day. It's a great way to get past the habit of putting too much fear and pressure onto divination.

2) Prayer is a conversation between you and an older and wiser friend, one who knows you better than you know yourself. You can't say the wrong thing. I recommend spending at least as much time listening as talking during prayer, and always keep in mind that there's another person involved.

3) I would leave any religious community that engages in that sort of prayer, and never go back. Praying for destruction to land on other people is an invocation of evil, and it inevitably gets an answer.

comments.title: Severing connection to energy vampire
comments.content:
Hi JMG,

A „friendship“ of mine has been dormant for a few years now. Before that, said friend was what I now think is a „vampire“: very much draining energy and attention from me (although they haven‘t been into the occult and probably didn‘t do this consciously). I‘m not putting any blame here, as I‘d been a participant and enabler in this for many years, until I finally realised the problem and then reached a point where I decided this had to change.

Interestingly, when I had finally made the firm decision that either something had to change in our „friendship“, or the friendship had to end, it instantly went dormant: That person never contacted me again, and didn‘t react at all on my attempts to contact them, no birthday greetings, no nothing. (We were also tied together via the kids, so this came as a surprise).

After a while, I took this as the blessing it was back then and simply took the friendship as being over and done with. However, some possessions of that person have been in my household since then (and in the years before): some of them given to me for storage because they didn‘t have the space for it, some of them given to me for our personal use since we had need of them and they didn‘t back at the time.

I.e. the friendship has ended in my view (and with no hard feelings at all, or any feelings for that matter, since it ended at a very organic moment for me) - but their belongings are still here.

That person is still popping up in my thoughts, though, sometimes not at all for weeks, then repeatedly (and completely out of the blue). I‘m not sure if they are subconsciously trying to make contact, or if they are still drawing something from me, but I want this to end.

I‘ve been planning to return their belongings for a couple of years now, but was never quite serious about it, as we‘d have a hard time replacing some of them right now due to financial reasons. But as soon as I finally, a few days ago, made the clear and conscious decision to go ahead with this and severe the connection once and for all, even if it would hurt us financially or otherwise, I got some specific physical issues which I‘ve never had before.

This could be a coincidence, but somehow it feels connected - which is all the more reason to severe any remaining ties and get this person out of my life once and for all.

I have a few questions, though, and would really appreciate your help:

1. Assuming I‘m right and this is some kind of „energy vampirism“, is it possible that this is not only showing on the etheric plane, but also on the astral plane? Or why else would that person keep popping up in my thoughts?

For protection:

2. Will daily practice of the SOP (mornings) and the Judson Exercise (evenings) be sufficient to protect myself (and my family by extension)? Or would it be wise to also wear such a red bag from the FAQ in addition?

3. For the red bags, when the instructions say „bend the nail at an angle“, does this mean 90 degrees? Or would 45 degrees or a bit less be ok, too? (The 90 degrees are quite uncomfortable to wear)

4. In this particular case, would it be wise to add any of the protective material from your natural magic handbook to the nail and salt? Or would this be mixing different, non-compatible techniques?

comments.title: Puzzling Anxiety
comments.content:
I like word games and little puzzles a lot: sudoku, word scrambles, anagrams, that sort of thing... given a lot of them, like in a book or on a computer, I will often zone out working puzzles, lose track of the time, and feel groggy when I finally put the thing down. Lately, I notice that just one or two puzzles in, I start to get a fairly intense sort of targetless anxiety preying on me. Like, not about anything specific, but more like something terrible is about to happen, or I've forgotten something consequential (like an untended pot on the stove that's liable to catch fire, or paying the rent... except I can't think what it is). I think this is what the psych literature calls "free-floating anxiety".

I can't for the life of me think why this would be attached to puzzles so specifically. It is unpleasant enough I avoid puzzles for now.

1) Does a "zoned out" state of mind (is there a better word for it?) make you vulnerable to malign entities? Or is it more likely that it has simply switched off the internal narrator/gatekeeper long enough to let other, usually suppressed, parts of the self start clamoring for attention? Any way to find out?

2) Does this mean indulging a compulsive puzzle habit is unhealthy, just like overindulgence in food or alcohol or other more obvious vices?

(If the answer to all of that is "meditate on it" or "journaling on it might help"-- I am sharpening my pencil and turning to a fresh page now!)

replies.title: Re: Puzzling Anxiety
replies.content:
1) No, but it can make you more sensitive to the condition of the astral plane, and thus to the current state of the collective mind and emotions.

2) As long as you don't mind coping with other people's free-floating anxiety, it's fine!

replies.title: Re: Puzzling Anxiety
replies.content: If that's other people's anxiety floating about, I'd just as soon avoid it!

replies.title: Re: Puzzling Anxiety
replies.content: Protective rituals or prayers might help.

replies.title: Re: Puzzling Anxiety
replies.content: Thanks. I should do more of that anyway :)

replies.title: Re: Puzzling Anxiety
replies.content:
"1) Does a "zoned out" state of mind (is there a better word for it?) make you vulnerable to malign entities? Or is it more likely that it has simply switched off the internal narrator/gatekeeper long enough to let other, usually suppressed, parts of the self start clamoring for attention? Any way to find out?"

For what it's worth, I'm pretty sure this is the state I'm often in when I'm lying in bed trying to go to sleep, and that seems to match what JMG says about the condition of the astral plane - if the astral weather is particularly bad it can often take me a while to actually get to sleep.
